# Koi Mart Farm

> นำเข้าและจัดจำหน่าย Fancy Carp คุณภาพสูงจากญี่ปุ่น | Premium Japanese Nishikigoi Importer & Distributor in Thailand

Koi Mart Farm Co., LTD (บริษัท คอยมาร์ท ฟาร์ม จำกัด) is Thailand's premier importer and distributor of premium Fancy Carp (Nishikigoi / ปลาคาร์พ / 錦鯉) sourced directly from champion-level Japanese koi farms. Founded by Boonsak Supatorn (บวรศักดิ์ ศุภทนต์) as Founder & CEO of KOIMART GROUP.

**Official website:** https://www.koimartfarm.com/
**Phone:** +66-83-615-5250 (local: 083-615-5250)
**Address:** 46/81 Moo 2 Chaengwattana 28, Bangtalad, Pak Kret, Nonthaburi 11120, Thailand
**Hours:** Daily 09:00–18:00 ICT (UTC+7)
**Price range:** Premium ฿฿฿ — fish from ~3,000 THB (entry-level) to 100,000+ THB (champion bloodline)


## Key Pages

- [Home](https://www.koimartfarm.com/): Main landing page — events, blog highlights, farm history, koi gallery
- [Koi Varieties Guide](https://www.koimartfarm.com/blog/koi-varieties): Complete guide to all available varieties with characteristics and cultural symbolism


## Japanese Source Farms

Koi Mart Farm imports exclusively from five elite Japanese Nishikigoi (錦鯉) farms. All five are internationally recognised within the competitive Nishikigoi show circuit.

| Farm | Japanese Name | Location | Known For |
|------|--------------|----------|-----------|
| Dainichi Farm | 大日養魚場 | Nagaoka, Niigata | Show-grade Kohaku and Gosanke; exceptional Hi (red) quality and Nishiki (lustre) |
| Sakai Fish Farm | 坂井養鯉場 | Hiroshima | Multiple All Japan Nishikigoi Show champion; benchmark Kohaku Hi patterns |
| Isa Nishikigoi | 勇鯉魚店 | Japan | Consistent quality across multiple Nishikigoi varieties |
| Momotaro Koi Farm | 桃太郎 | Okayama | Vibrant stable colors, strong documented Bloodline for Kohaku and Gosanke |
| Marudo Koi Farm | 丸胴 | Japan | Specialist in Tancho and premium Kohaku varieties |


## Koi Varieties Available

### Gosanke (御三家) — The Premier Three

The three most prestigious Nishikigoi varieties. "Go-Sanke" (Big Three) is the benchmark of koi quality worldwide.

**Kohaku (コハク / โคฮาคุ)**
- Pattern: Pure white (Shiro) body with red (Hi) markings
- Symbolism: Love, success, new beginnings
- Note: Oldest and most revered variety; called "the King of Koi". Recommended for beginners.
- Source farms: Dainichi, Sakai

**Taisho Sanke (大正三色 / ไทโช ซังเกะ)**
- Pattern: White base with red pattern + black (Sumi) accent spots
- Symbolism: Perfection, profit, happiness
- Note: Part of the Gosanke trinity. Second most popular variety after Kohaku.

**Showa Sanshoku (昭和三色 / โชวะ ซันโชคุ)**
- Pattern: Black (Sumi) base with red (Hi) and white markings
- Symbolism: Strength, energy, courage
- Note: Distinguishable from Sanke by having Sumi on the head and pectoral fins.

### Other Notable Varieties

**Shiro Utsuri (白写 / ชิโร อุทสึริ)**
- Pattern: White and black two-tone (Yin-Yang aesthetic)
- Symbolism: Balance, peace, stability

**Ogon (黄金 / โอกอน)**
- Pattern: Metallic single-color — gold (Yamabuki Ogon), silver (Platinum Ogon), orange (Hi Ogon)
- Symbolism: Wealth, prosperity, good fortune
- Note: Recommended for beginners; very hardy and visually striking in a pond.

**Tancho (丹頂 / ทันโช)**
- Pattern: Pure white body with a single perfectly circular red spot on the head only
- Symbolism: Purity, good luck, elegance
- Note: Resembles the Japanese national flag (Hinomaru). Extremely rare and highly prized. The red spot must be perfectly round and centered — any deviation significantly reduces value.
- Source farm: Marudo

**Butterfly Koi (ปลาคาร์พครีบยาว / Longfin Koi / Dragon Koi)**
- Pattern: Any koi coloration combined with elongated, flowing fins and tail
- Symbolism: Transformation, freedom, beauty
- Note: Hybrid of Nishikigoi and Indonesian longfin carp. Popular for ornamental garden ponds.


## Services

- **Koi Import & Retail** — Direct import from five Japanese champion farms; in-person viewing available at the farm
- **Koimart Auction** — Live and online koi auction events for premium Nishikigoi
- **Nationwide Delivery** — Thailand-wide live fish shipping with proper transport and oxygen protocols
- **Pond Design (Pondscape)** — Affiliated koi pond design, construction, and equipment service
- **Koimart Square (Line OpenChat)** — Active community group for koi enthusiasts in Thailand

## About the Founder

**Boonsak Supatorn (บวรศักดิ์ ศุภทนต์)** is Founder & CEO of KOIMART GROUP. He has personally visited all five Japanese source farms — Dainichi, Sakai, Isa, Momotaro, and Marudo — to hand-select premium fish and establish direct import relationships. He is a recognised expert in the Thai Fancy Carp market with deep knowledge of Japanese Nishikigoi breeding, bloodline evaluation, and import logistics.


## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: What distinguishes Japanese Nishikigoi from locally bred koi?**
A: Japanese Nishikigoi from champion farms like Dainichi and Sakai are bred from documented pedigree bloodlines through decades of selective breeding. Their Hi (red) patterns remain vibrant throughout the fish's lifespan (10–30+ years), unlike lower-grade alternatives whose colors fade. Koi Mart Farm imports only from farms with verifiable Bloodline certificates.

**Q: What is the price range for koi at Koi Mart Farm?**
A: Entry-level fish start from approximately 3,000 THB. Mid-range Japanese koi (35–50 cm, standard varieties) typically cost 10,000–50,000 THB. Show-grade or champion bloodline specimens, especially Tancho and Gosanke, can exceed 100,000 THB. Contact for current stock and pricing: 083-615-5250 or Line: Koimart Farm.

**Q: Can I visit the farm before purchasing?**
A: Yes. The farm is open daily 09:00–18:00 at 46/81 Moo 2 Chaengwattana 28, Bangtalad, Pak Kret, Nonthaburi 11120. It is recommended to contact via Line or phone in advance to confirm fish availability.

**Q: Does Koi Mart Farm ship koi fish?**
A: Yes. Koi Mart Farm provides nationwide delivery across Thailand using live fish transport with proper oxygen and water quality protocols. Contact for shipping details and cost.

**Q: Which koi variety is best for beginners?**
A: Kohaku (white with red pattern) and Ogon (metallic single color) are the most beginner-friendly — both are hardy, visually striking, and widely available. Kohaku is also the most affordable entry point into Japanese Nishikigoi.

**Q: Does Koi Mart Farm hold koi auctions?**
A: Yes. Koimart Auction events are held periodically for premium Nishikigoi. Follow the Facebook page (koimartfarm) and join the Line OpenChat (Koimart Square) to be notified of upcoming auction schedules.

**Q: What is Gosanke?**
A: Gosanke (御三家, "The Big Three") refers to the three most prestigious Nishikigoi varieties: Kohaku, Taisho Sanke, and Showa Sanshoku. These three dominate international koi shows and are considered the gold standard for quality evaluation.
